jokes.text: I ain't tippin' some jerk two bucks to tie a TV to my car. Mama Moe didn't raise no fools.
jokes.setup: Store employee offers to help tie TV to car
jokes.punchline: Homer's cheap logic and 'Mama Moe' reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cheapskate behavior with good character voice

jokes.text: Well, I guess I can just keep watching DVDs by following family vans around.
jokes.setup: Homer refuses help tying TV to car
jokes.punchline: Absurd solution of following other cars to watch their screens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that feels perfectly Homer - original and economical

jokes.text: Big Bird in China? Won't Grover get lonely? Oh, no, wait. He's hangin' out with Derek Jeter.
jokes.setup: Homer watching DVD on someone else's car screen
jokes.punchline: Random connection between Sesame Street and Derek Jeter
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity reference feels a bit cheap but fits Homer's stream of consciousness

jokes.text: Now ignore all the fancy shmancy things thingamajigs, boy.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to focus Bart in electronics store
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up descriptive language
jokes.explanation: Good character voice but fairly standard Homer malapropism

jokes.text: Care to make love, sir?
jokes.setup: Aggressive sales pitches escalating
jokes.punchline: Unexpected romantic proposition mixed with sales pitches
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and economy but could be any character

jokes.text: That'll be 50 cents, or free with the purchase of a $200 TiVO plus a two-year contract. Sold!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately wants just a battery
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls for terrible deal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer impulsiveness - great character consistency

jokes.text: Come on, Lisa! The TV's been off for five whole minutes!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he forgot about TiVO installation
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated desperation about brief TV downtime
jokes.explanation: Good Homer TV addiction characterization with nice escalation

jokes.text: I spit on your corpse, advertisers supporting television.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers he can skip commercials
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic declaration of war on advertisers
jokes.explanation: Homer's theatrical over-reaction feels fresh and character-specific

jokes.text: TiVo, you've got a season pass to my heart.
jokes.setup: Homer praising TiVO technology
jokes.punchline: Romantic declaration using TiVO terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that feels earned and quotable

jokes.text: My boyfriend Straightman just proposed...that we go on separate vacations again this year!
jokes.setup: Talk show host announces boyfriend proposed
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation - proposed separate vacations, not marriage
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection joke with good timing

jokes.text: I saved Lost, watched all Rome in a day, and got Two and a half men in two and a half minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his TiVO accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Escalating time compression ending with perfect math pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect mathematical punchline - very quotable

jokes.text: Then I ran out of space and had to choose between Sophie's Choice and Schindler's list. Why is everything Hollywood make so excellent?
jokes.setup: Homer describing storage dilemma
jokes.punchline: Casually treating Holocaust films as entertainment options, then praising Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that works through Homer's obliviousness to the weight of the films

jokes.text: Keith Olbermann?! That's right, content burglar Marge Simpson.
jokes.setup: Marge surprised by Olbermann's appearance
jokes.punchline: Olbermann's dramatic accusation of 'content burglary'
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity parody with appropriate dramatic flair

jokes.text: You've been watching TV shows, but skipping the commercials that pay for them. That makes you...the worst person in the world!
jokes.setup: Olbermann explaining Marge's crime
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dramatic escalation to 'worst person in the world'
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of Olbermann's dramatic style and hyperbole

jokes.text: My cousin Marine saw you flying in business class! I was upgraded against my will!
jokes.setup: Marge accusing TV personalities of having money
jokes.punchline: Olbermann's defensive excuse about unwanted upgrade
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing celebrity defensiveness

jokes.text: It takes a team of 20 people to keep me that way, without ad money, I'd look like a swamp monster from a child's nightmare!
jokes.setup: Olbermann explaining why ads are important
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of appearance without makeup team
jokes.explanation: Colorful imagery that works as celebrity self-roast

jokes.text: And under here, I look even worse. Then it gets better, then even worse!
jokes.setup: Olbermann removing layers of makeup/prosthetics
jokes.punchline: Rollercoaster of appearance quality
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that relies on animation but dialogue supports the bit

jokes.text: That's why this is the only mop endorsed by me, former NFL draft pick, Ryan Leaf.
jokes.setup: Celebrity endorsement for cleaning product
jokes.punchline: Ryan Leaf, famous NFL bust, endorsing mops
jokes.explanation: Sharp sports reference connecting failed quarterback to cleaning products

jokes.text: Their commercial featured a talking frog. So tell those idiots in the State Capital to let Union Carbide do their thing.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she supports Prop 87
jokes.punchline: Supporting environmental destruction because of cute mascot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of how advertising manipulates through cute imagery - very sharp

jokes.text: Paid for by Cartoon Frogs For Wetlands Destruction.
jokes.setup: Political commercial disclaimer
jokes.punchline: Absurdly honest organization name
jokes.explanation: Perfect satirical organization name - extremely quotable and economical

jokes.text: Sometimes I think about gettin' on a bus and never comin' back.
jokes.setup: Commercial asking about family dinner arguments
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprisingly dark response about abandoning family
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark Homer moment that feels authentic to his character frustrations

jokes.text: Good luck with my finicky appetite. We've got ribs. Sold!
jokes.setup: Lisa presents herself as difficult to please
jokes.punchline: Immediately satisfied by simplest option
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Lisa's usual principled stance

jokes.text: More ribs for me! More ribs for me! More ribs for me?
jokes.setup: Family pointing out restaurant problems
jokes.punchline: Homer's consistent optimism degrading to uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's optimism slowly cracking

jokes.text: And 'Wes Doobner's World Famous Family Style Rib Huts' is actually an anagram! Welcome to Sideshow Bob's World Famous Family Style Return!
jokes.setup: Bob reveals his trap
jokes.punchline: Restaurant name is elaborate anagram
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay trap that perfectly suits Bob's intellectual villainy - very clever construction

jokes.text: I'd like to know if Wes Doobner is aware of what you're doing in his restaurant. I'm Wes Doobner.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about restaurant owner
jokes.punchline: Bob matter-of-factly reveals he IS the owner
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of tension through Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: You never ask what we've been up to. We went to the strawberry patch and I picked the most strawberries!
jokes.setup: Bob about to launch into villain monologue
jokes.punchline: Bart interrupts with innocent child sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that deflates villain theatrics with child innocence

jokes.text: Just call me...Slideshow Bob!
jokes.setup: Bob presenting visual aids for his story
jokes.punchline: Pun on his name with presentation format
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that fits Bob's theatrical personality perfectly

jokes.text: Nobody do it.
jokes.setup: Bob makes 'Slideshow Bob' pun and pauses for reaction
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan instruction to family not to react
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy moment - Bart denying Bob his theatrical satisfaction is perfectly economical

jokes.text: I snuck into America amidst a bunch of undocumented Canadian comedy writers for The Jimmy Kimmel Show, whatever that is.
jokes.setup: Bob explaining how he returned to America
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about competing late-night show
jokes.explanation: Good industry joke that fits Bob's intellectual snobbery

jokes.text: Which one? 'The Hills Have Eyes Three: The Hills Still Have Eyes'.
jokes.setup: Bob mentions directing offer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous horror sequel title
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of generic horror sequel naming conventions

jokes.text: Actually, you made one. What Shakespeare really said was, 'Twere well it were done quickly.'
jokes.setup: Bob misquotes Shakespeare confidently
jokes.punchline: Lisa corrects his Shakespeare quote
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lisa's knowledge trumping Bob's pretension is very satisfying

jokes.text: Come on, Wikipedia, load, you unwieldy behemoth.
jokes.setup: Bob checking Shakespeare quote online
jokes.punchline: Frustrated plea to slow website
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern frustration delivered in Bob's theatrical style

jokes.text: It's 'hoist with his own petard.' Oh, get a life.
jokes.setup: Lisa corrects Bob's Shakespeare again
jokes.punchline: Bob's deflated dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Bob's intellectual pride being wounded repeatedly

jokes.text: Young? I'm the oldest kid in my class! By like two years.
jokes.setup: Bob calls Bart young
jokes.punchline: Bart's defensive response reveals he's been held back
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that explains Bart's academic struggles humorously

jokes.text: Did they name the Toyota Cressida after the play, or the play after the car? Play first, then car. I just lost a thousand dollars.
jokes.setup: Judge asks about Toyota Cressida naming
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had a bet on the answer
jokes.explanation: Absurd non sequitur that shows judge's inappropriate gambling priorities

jokes.text: He ordered prank pizzas to 888 Poopy Pants Lane! Poopy Pants Lane ends in the 700 block!
jokes.setup: Townsperson complaining about Bart's pranks
jokes.punchline: Reveals intimate knowledge of fake address street layout
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail - someone knowing the street layout of a fake address is perfect

jokes.text: He cheats at Uno!
jokes.setup: List of Bart's crimes building
jokes.punchline: Mundane card game cheating among serious accusations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation capper that deflates the serious tone

jokes.text: Nitroglycerin! Everybody panic! Yoink! Foiled again, freak!
jokes.setup: Bob threatening everyone with explosive
jokes.punchline: Bart casually steals it without fear
jokes.explanation: Good action sequence with Bart's fearless response to Bob's theatrics

jokes.text: Ew, gross--I'm touching a dead guy!
jokes.setup: Bart grabbing 'dead' Bob
jokes.punchline: Childish grossed-out reaction to touching corpse
jokes.explanation: Perfect child reaction that maintains Bart's character in dramatic moment

jokes.text: America has a tradition of turning outlaws into legends after their deaths: Billy the Kid, Bonnie and Clyde, Jesus Christ.
jokes.setup: News anchor listing American outlaw legends
jokes.punchline: Including Jesus Christ in list of outlaws
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive escalation that's both shocking and historically accurate

jokes.text: A veritable who's who ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Describing clowns at funeral
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'who's who' with clown laughter
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works with the funeral context

jokes.text: Bart stole your nitroglycerin And then your heart infarcked
jokes.setup: Funeral song about Bob's death
jokes.punchline: Using medical term 'infarcted' to rhyme awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good awkward rhyme that captures amateur funeral song quality

jokes.text: Like when you fell and split your pants And we saw your 'Frank and Beans.'
jokes.setup: Song about DVD extras of Bob's sketches
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate reference to exposed genitals at funeral
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that works through inappropriateness of funeral setting

jokes.text: There's the kid who killed my best friend! Buy my DVD.
jokes.setup: Krusty confronting Bart at funeral
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to commercial pitch
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Krusty's commercialism overriding grief

jokes.text: Yeah, well, between you and me, I still can't stand him.
jokes.setup: Family suggesting Bart should pay respects
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest admission of continued hatred for dead enemy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart honesty - refusing to be fake nice even at a funeral

jokes.text: Church jerks... church jerks... Anus, anus... anus, anus, anus...
jokes.setup: Homer at solemn funeral trying to be respectful
jokes.punchline: Can't stop making childish word associations
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer immaturity perfectly timed for maximum inappropriateness

jokes.text: That summer when he wanted to tour the castles of Italy, I wanted to tour the castles of France. So we went to Spain, a compromise that satisfied no one.
jokes.setup: Cecil explaining his relationship with Bob
jokes.punchline: Terrible compromise that pleases nobody
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-defining joke that captures dysfunctional sibling dynamic perfectly

jokes.text: That's stupid. You talk like youre smart, but you're stupid.
jokes.setup: Cecil giving intellectual explanation
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment cuts through pretension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cutting through adult BS with childish honesty

jokes.text: Ball one, ball two, strike one...
jokes.setup: Milhouse showing off umpire counter to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart has already left, Milhouse talking to nobody
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's social cluelessness

jokes.text: Feet... killing?! We've got to save Bart!
jokes.setup: Milhouse casually mentions his feet are killing him
jokes.punchline: Lisa makes connection to danger for Bart
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay revelation that shows Lisa's detective skills

jokes.text: I came here to tell you I never meant for you to die. I just wanted you to go to jail and get beat up a lot.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make peace with dead Bob
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that he wanted Bob to suffer, just not die
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart honesty - dark but somehow innocent in its directness

jokes.text: Then let's give the people what they want!
jokes.setup: Bart says everyone wants him dead
jokes.punchline: Bob's theatrical agreement to kill him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob theatrical villainy with populist twist

jokes.text: Cool, I found a trapdoor that leads to an underground city. Here I go!
jokes.setup: Family looking for Bart in dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Bart casually exploring obviously dangerous underground area
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart adventurous obliviousness to danger

jokes.text: Cool, I found a trapdoor that leads to an underground city. Here I go!
jokes.setup: Family searching for Bart in dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited distraction by unrelated discovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his distractibility even during family crisis

jokes.text: Yes, and Hamlet is all about Laertes. Would you please stop comparing me to Laertes!
jokes.setup: Cecil claiming credit for the plan
jokes.punchline: Bob's frustration at being compared to secondary character
jokes.explanation: Great sibling rivalry joke using Shakespeare knowledge as weapon

jokes.text: Yes, and Hamlet is all about Laertes. Would you please stop comparing me to Laertes! If the doublet fits...
jokes.setup: Brother rivalry over Shakespearean character comparisons
jokes.punchline: Theatrical pun on 'if the shoe fits'
jokes.explanation: Good literary humor showing family rivalry through Shakespeare references

jokes.text: Zeus' pimples! Hot! Hot! Hot!
jokes.setup: Homer encountering crematory heat
jokes.punchline: Classical oath combined with slapstick reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent character exclamation with classical reference, though somewhat random

jokes.text: I grew suspicious when I saw the casket had extra room built-in for your feet. Why would your family pay all that extra money of a dead man? Damn these glorious gunboats!
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how she solved the mystery
jokes.punchline: Bob's vanity about his large feet gave him away
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that uses established character trait as plot device - very satisfying

jokes.text: You stink like my butt!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to think of final clever line
jokes.punchline: Completely childish and illogical insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-climactic Bart line that deflates dramatic moment appropriately

jokes.text: You stink like my butt! Take him away! Oh, why must I feed him straight lines?!
jokes.setup: Bart delivering weak insult to defeated Bob
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's meta-complaint about being comedy straight man
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with Wiggum breaking fourth wall about comedy dynamics

jokes.text: Oh, why must I feed him straight lines?!
jokes.setup: Bob asking rhetorical question
jokes.punchline: Self-aware complaint about his role in comedy dynamic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy moment - Bob aware of his comedy function is perfect character writing

jokes.text: Slash! Hack! Die!
jokes.setup: Homer cutting celebratory cake
jokes.punchline: Using violent language while doing innocent activity
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of violent language with domestic activity

jokes.text: The joke's not funny and the bid's not sufficient.
jokes.setup: Prison card game scene
jokes.punchline: Inmate critiques both comedy and bridge simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line that works on multiple levels - economical and smart
